reviews.review_msg: Had a great time in his class. Explained concepts clearly & his lecture notes were very useful
Boxin in pretty good. He gives you four really easy marks with the midterms, but study hard for the final.
If you don't have any friends study with you, don't skip because he did not post his notes on canvas.

Sometimes the accent is hard to understand, but great prof though. Goes through problems in class. Also there was 4 midterms (which I think is a good thing because a screw up on one midterm doesn't tank your grade).

Do & understand ALL the questions in textbook; theyre tougher than the midterms. Do his practice midterms, theyre similar to the actual. Final exam is tough; 1/3 of the questions were for the last 2 chapters of the book. Although you can self-learn from the text, attend lectures since he doesnt post notes, which have useful tips/tricks for exams
this course scared me at first, but once you understand the concept, very easy

Excellent prof with great explanations and lots of in class examples. Would highly recommend sitting in the front few rows due to his soft speaking voice. He has as a bit of accent but not hard to understand at all. Midterms are very fair but the final was somewhat difficult.
Good lecturer although he can be hard to understand at times. Tests are pretty straightforward. Dry sense of humour is awesome.

Boxin is a very fair professor. He's sometimes a little unclear in lecture because he's ESL and soft spoken, but mostly does a good job. Wish the course was a bit more organized (no notes or practice questions).
Did a great job covering everything without any surprises on the tests and homework. It's also a great course to take in general. Would heavily recommend him.

Fantastic professor, super clear on most of the material, but he is difficult to understand at first (accent + softly spoken) - that's not a big deal though. Exams were incredibly straightforward, slightly easier than homework questions. The text is also solid to read if you're unclear. All I did was understand each homework question and got an A+

As a Chinese professor, his accent is not heavy which you can understand what he is talking about. He likes to skip steps on examples, but if you visit him during the office hour, or ask his TA, you should be okay, as this course is not that difficult as expected.
He's a really nice informative prof. One of my favorite profs in sfu.

This man is amazing. His knowledge of statistics is vast and varied. Boxin's teaching style is very clear, but sometimes he likes to omit steps. Nonetheless, his talent is inspiring and his love for the subject has allowed him to so easily teach it. His exams might require some creativity, but are straightforward in many cases. Would recommend
really a good prof. His voice is a lil bit low in lecture but if u ask him for hellp after class or in office hour, he will help you a lot by making those cufused stuff clear to you.

If you listen and follow his notes, you should be able to do very well.  He is a very fair professor.  He doesn't speak very loud, but if you get his notes, you don't miss anything.  Show up for class, read over his notes, and he is one of the most fair teachers out there!! :):) And he has a dry humor occasionally.

He mumbles a lot which makes it hard to listen even when you sit at the front. His class can be very boring, but his notes are awesome! One drawback is he uses different notations from the text, which can be confusing. His exams are pretty straightforward, assignment types. He keeps his words on lowering midterm worth when u did better on final.
a good prof, and he;s always ready to help his students. exams are well related to the course notes.
The easiest courses I have ever had
Good prof in general.Easy course and crystal note. But you have to sit in the front of class or you cant hear him say a word!

Very light class.  The only work done in this class consists of preparatory assignments for the two midterms and the final.  Be sure you know how to get your sum of squares by deriving them from the models.

Sit near the front or you won't be able to hear him. Doesn't allow a formula sheet (and there are a number of them), but the exams were okay. They weren't out of a lot, so not much room for mistakes. Try to memorize formulas for the final. Homework was not bad and infrequent. Easier to understand than stat 270, in my opinion.

Boxin is great. Super good at what he does. He is a genius when it comes to stats. Really fair and straightforward tests. Only thing is, he talks really quietly so he is sometimes hard to understand. Great prof tho.

I have strong respect to his knowledge. I was fortunate enough to be the only attendant of his office hours. His is easy-going, knows everything about his course and stat and even the way to study. Every one should have met such professor at his/her early age to help make decision on study and even carrer. Exams are fair.
good,very helpful

My deep respect to this man of knowledge. I was fortunate enough to be almost single attendant of his office hours during the semester that inspired and taught me a lot. His attitude is receptive, phrases are finished, focus is steady, connection with book is clear, exams well announced and predictable. Boris.
He may be smart, but he really needs to improve his teaching and marking in the following areas: effectiveness, clarity, and marking, etc.

He knows his stuff but understanding what he's saying in lectures is hard. His English is heavily accented and he always sounds like he's mumbling. The book is crap because it's focused on applications. Go to his office hrs if u want to learn and suck it up when he accuses u of not going to class.

Good improvement in teaching,lecture notes. Good use of microphone and overhead (which he never used before). Good to see that he was willing to provide so many extra office hours, which is rarely seen in the Stat Dept! However, it would be even better if the lecture notes were more organized. Sometimes they were a bit hard to follow.
The worst teacher i ever had..wasting time on handing over the hwk by calling everyone's name...and he doesn't speak english at all...he should goto ESL before coming to lecture....

I agree with the above -- I felt duped into thinking this was an extension of 302.  The prerequisite said 285 OR 302.  302 is a joke stats class, and did not prepare me for 402.  I asked for help with the math, explicitly indicating I did not have matrix math. I was told to drop the course, but if I did, I would be forfeiting a grad school offer :(

Nice prof. Great at explaining material, especially one-on-one. Very slow going, and questions are encouraged. MTs are easy (i averaged 90% with small effort) but the finals are hard, study for them thoroughly.
Nice and decent prof. It is seldom having a prof whose teaching pace is so slow. It's nice just laying back in his class. Fair easy MTs but finals are HARD. I get B- for both with average efforts.
Great prof! he teaches the material realli slow and clear, it is just that he speaak so soft and it is realli hard to hear what he said. MTs are easy, but final is hard!

im sure this guy is really smart, but I dont think he is speaking English.. is he?  Can anyone else understand a word that comes out of his mouth?  Even the translator for the deff boy didnt get it and was writing "inaudible... mumbling...incomprehensible..."  Thank goodness for the ... text book?

Lots unexplained math "myth" in his lecture. Tutorial and lecture are totally unrelated. Exam is like everyone can do 60% of the very easy question, only a few of us can figure out the rest. That means almost everyone got average.

Very helpful, but unfortunately very unfair judgment at the end. Its hard to demonstrate you know the material when you can't do the math in the course ... math that SHOULD BE declared via a pre-requisite requirement: MATH 232.
